---
title: "File Formats, Parquet & DuckDB"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Source+Code+Pro:ital,wght@0,300;0,400;0,500;0,600;0,700;0,900;1,400&display=swap');
/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   LIGHT-BLUE THEME — soft blue canvas, Source Code Pro
   tables w/ royal-blue headers, orange + green as
   the only highlight accents
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */

/* Palette tokens
   --bg            #e9f1fd   page background (light blue)
   --bg-2          #f3f8ff   raised panel / right column
   --panel         #dde9fb   sticky-left visual stage
   --surface       #ffffff   cards / tables
   --line          #c4d4ee   hairline borders
   --ink           #16263f   primary text
   --ink-dim       #5b6b86   secondary text
   --blue          #3b62db   table header / accent
   --blue-ink      #2747b0   strong blue for text
   --orange        #f2a159   highlight A
   --green         #4ab87d   highlight B
   --cell-ink      #16181d   table cell text */

*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }

body {
  font-family: 'Source Code Pro', ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, monospace;
  background: #e9f1fd;
  color: #16263f;
  overflow-x: hidden;
  line-height: 1.65;
  -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
}

/* Kill Quarto containers */
main.content, #quarto-content, .page-columns,
.page-columns .content, .content, #quarto-document-content,
.quarto-title-block { max-width: none !important; width: 100% !important; margin: 0 !important; padding: 0 !important; }
.quarto-title-block { display: none; }

/* ── PROGRESS BAR ── */
#progress-bar {
  position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; height: 2px;
  background: #3b62db;
  width: 0%; z-index: 9999;
}

/* ── SIDE NAV ── */
#side-nav {
  position: fixed; right: 1.4rem; top: 50%; transform: translateY(-50%);
  display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.55rem; z-index: 999;
}
.nav-dot {
  width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%;
  background: transparent; border: 1.5px solid #9bb4e0;
  cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.2s; position: relative;
}
.nav-dot:hover, .nav-dot.active { background: #3b62db; border-color: #3b62db; transform: scale(1.4); }
.nav-tip {
  position: absolute; right: 1.5rem; top: 50%; transform: translateY(-50%);
  background: #16263f; color: #eaf1fc; border: 1px solid #2747b0;
  padding: 0.2rem 0.55rem;
  border-radius: 4px; font-size: 0.7rem; white-space: nowrap;
  opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; transition: opacity 0.18s;
}
.nav-dot:hover .nav-tip { opacity: 1; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   HERO — light blue, mono type
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.hero {
  min-height: 100vh; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  text-align: center; padding: 4rem 2rem; position: relative; overflow: hidden;
  background: linear-gradient(180deg, #dceafb 0%, #e9f1fd 100%);
  border-bottom: 1px solid #c4d4ee;
}
.hero-grid-bg {
  position: absolute; inset: 0; z-index: 0;
  background-image:
    linear-gradient(rgba(59,98,219,0.06) 1px, transparent 1px),
    linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(59,98,219,0.06) 1px, transparent 1px);
  background-size: 48px 48px;
}
.hero-glow { display: none; }
.hero-inner { position: relative; z-index: 1; max-width: 820px; }
.hero-eyebrow {
  display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem;
  background: rgba(59,98,219,0.08); border: 1px solid #b6c9ee;
  color: #2747b0; padding: 0.35rem 1rem; border-radius: 999px;
  font-size: 0.72rem; letter-spacing: 0.16em; text-transform: uppercase;
  margin-bottom: 2rem;
}
.hero h1 {
  font-size: clamp(2.7rem, 6.5vw, 5.5rem); font-weight: 700; color: #16263f;
  line-height: 1.0; letter-spacing: -0.03em; margin-bottom: 1.8rem;
}
.hero h1 em { color: #3b62db; font-style: normal; }
.hero-sub {
  font-size: 1.0rem; color: #5b6b86; max-width: 600px; margin: 0 auto 3.5rem;
  line-height: 1.75;
}
.hero-pills { display: flex; gap: 0.6rem; justify-content: center; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 3rem; }
.hero-pill {
  padding: 0.4rem 1rem; border-radius: 999px; font-size: 0.78rem;
  font-weight: 500; border: 1px solid #b6c9ee; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.5);
}
.hero-pill-1 { color: #2747b0; }
.hero-pill-2 { color: #2747b0; }
.hero-pill-3 { color: #5b6b86; }
.hero-pill-4 { color: #5b6b86; }
.hero-scroll-cue {
  color: #7185a3; font-size: 0.74rem; letter-spacing: 0.12em;
  text-transform: uppercase; animation: bob 2s ease-in-out infinite;
}
.hero-scroll-cue span { display: block; font-size: 1.6rem; color: #3b62db; margin-top: 0.3rem; }
@keyframes bob { 0%,100%{transform:translateY(0)} 50%{transform:translateY(8px)} }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   CHAPTER INTROS — light strips, blue rule
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.chapter-intro {
  padding: 5rem 4rem 3rem;
  border-top: 1px solid #3b62db;
  display: flex; align-items: flex-end; gap: 3rem;
  background: linear-gradient(180deg, #dceafb 0%, #e9f1fd 100%);
}
.chapter-intro .ch-num {
  font-size: clamp(5rem,10vw,9rem); font-weight: 700; line-height: 1;
  color: rgba(59,98,219,0.14); letter-spacing: -0.05em; flex-shrink: 0;
}
.chapter-intro .ch-text .ch-tag {
  font-size: 0.72rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.18em;
  color: #2747b0; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
}
.chapter-intro .ch-text h2 {
  font-size: clamp(1.8rem,3.6vw,2.9rem); font-weight: 600; color: #16263f;
  letter-spacing: -0.02em; line-height: 1.15;
}
.chapter-intro .ch-text p {
  color: #5b6b86; font-size: 0.98rem; line-height: 1.7; max-width: 60ch; margin-top: 0.8rem;
}

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   STICKY SCROLLY LAYOUT
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.sticky-scrolly {
  display: flex;
  background: #e9f1fd;
}
.sticky-left {
  position: sticky; top: 0; height: 100vh;
  width: 50%; flex-shrink: 0;
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  padding: 2rem;
  border-right: 1px solid #c4d4ee;
  background: #dde9fb;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.sticky-right {
  flex: 1; padding: 0 3.5rem 20vh 3.5rem;
  background: #f3f8ff;
}

/* Visual panels inside sticky-left */
.sticky-panel {
  position: absolute; inset: 0;
  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  padding: 1.75rem;
  opacity: 0; transition: opacity 0.4s ease, transform 0.4s ease;
  transform: translateY(14px);
  pointer-events: none;
  overflow-y: auto;
}
.sticky-panel.active {
  opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); pointer-events: auto;
}
.sticky-panel > div { width: 100%; max-width: 460px; }

/* Step blocks in sticky-right */
.step {
  min-height: 80vh; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center;
  padding: 5rem 0;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #d3e0f4;
  opacity: 0.32; transition: opacity 0.4s;
}
.step.is-active { opacity: 1; }
.step:last-child { border-bottom: none; }
.step-tag {
  font-size: 0.7rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.18em;
  color: #2747b0; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 0.8rem;
}
.step h3 {
  font-size: clamp(1.4rem, 2.4vw, 2rem); font-weight: 600; color: #16263f;
  letter-spacing: -0.01em; line-height: 1.2; margin-bottom: 1.1rem;
}
.step p { color: #33445f; font-size: 0.97rem; line-height: 1.8; margin-bottom: 0.9rem; }
.step p:last-of-type { margin-bottom: 1.2rem; }
.step code {
  background: #dde9fb; border: 1px solid #b6c9ee;
  color: #2747b0; padding: 0.1em 0.4em; border-radius: 4px; font-size: 0.88em;
}

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   BADGES — outline chips, two accent colors
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.badge-row { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.45rem; margin-top: 0.8rem; }
.badge {
  padding: 0.28rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 999px; font-size: 0.72rem;
  font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; border: 1px solid #b6c9ee;
  background: #ffffff; color: #33445f;
}
.bi { color: #2747b0; border-color: #b6c9ee; background: #eef3fe; }
.bs { color: #2747b0; border-color: #b6c9ee; background: #eef3fe; }
.bg { color: #2c7a52; border-color: #abdcc3; background: #e9f8f0; }
.ba { color: #b9692a; border-color: #f2cba3; background: #fdf0e3; }
.br { color: #b9692a; border-color: #f2cba3; background: #fdf0e3; }
.bp { color: #5b6b86; border-color: #c4d4ee; background: #ffffff; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   SQL / CODE BLOCKS — dark navy, the one dark element
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.sql-block {
  background: #11244a; border: 1px solid #1d3a6e;
  border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.95rem 1.2rem;
  font-family: 'Source Code Pro', monospace;
  font-size: 0.78rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #d8e3f7;
  margin: 0.8rem 0; overflow-x: auto;
}
.sql-block .kw { color: #8fb0ff; font-weight: 600; }
.sql-block .ty { color: #6ee7b7; }
.sql-block .fn { color: #ffffff; }
.sql-block .st { color: #f2a159; }
.sql-block .cm { color: #7f93b8; font-style: italic; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   FILE FORMAT COMPARISON TABLE
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.vis-table {
  width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse;
  border: 1px solid #c4cbe0;
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  font-family: 'Source Code Pro', monospace;
  background: #ffffff;
}
.vis-table th {
  background: #3b62db; color: #ffffff; padding: 0.6rem 1rem;
  font-weight: 600; text-align: left; font-size: 0.8rem;
  letter-spacing: 0.02em; border: 1px solid #3b62db;
  text-transform: none;
}
.vis-table td {
  padding: 0.55rem 1rem; border: 1px solid #d2d6e2;
  color: #16181d; background: #ffffff; text-align: left; word-break: break-word;
}
.vis-table tr:nth-child(even) td { background: #ffffff; }
.cell-good { color: #2c7a52; font-weight: 600; }
.cell-bad  { color: #b9692a; font-weight: 600; }
.cell-mid  { color: #b9692a; font-weight: 600; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   ROW vs COLUMN vs HYBRID VISUAL
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.layout-compare {
  display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 1rem; width: 100%;
}
.layout-block { border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid #c4cbe0; }
.layout-head {
  padding: 0.5rem 1rem; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 700;
  text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.1em;
}
.layout-head.row-h   { background: #fdf0e3; color: #b9692a; border-bottom: 1px solid #f2cba3; }
.layout-head.col-h   { background: #e9f8f0; color: #2c7a52; border-bottom: 1px solid #abdcc3; }
.layout-head.hyb-h   { background: #eef3fe; color: #2747b0; border-bottom: 1px solid #b6c9ee; }
.layout-grid {
  display: grid; padding: 0.5rem;
  gap: 3px;
}
.lg-row { grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); }
.lg-col { grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); }
.lg-hyb { grid-template-columns: repeat(8, 1fr); }
.cell {
  border-radius: 4px; padding: 0.28rem 0.1rem;
  font-size: 0.62rem; text-align: center; font-weight: 600;
}
.c-a { background: #d6e2fb; color: #2747b0; }
.c-b { background: #cdeedd; color: #1f6443; }
.c-c { background: #fbe3cd; color: #8a4a1c; }
.c-d { background: #dfe7f8; color: #3a4d80; }
.c-g { background: #e7edf7; color: #5b6b86; font-style: italic; }
.rg-sep {
  grid-column: 1 / -1; height: 1px; background: #d3e0f4; margin: 2px 0;
}

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   PARQUET STRUCTURE VISUAL
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.parquet-struct {
  width: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0;
  border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid #1d3a6e;
  font-size: 0.78rem;
}
.pq-magic {
  background: #11244a; color: #8fb0ff; padding: 0.4rem 1rem;
  font-family: 'Source Code Pro', monospace; font-weight: 700; text-align: center;
  font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.2em;
}
.pq-rg {
  background: #f3f8ff; border-top: 1px solid #c4d4ee;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #c4d4ee;
}
.pq-rg-label {
  background: #3b62db; color: #fff; padding: 0.3rem 1rem;
  font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.1em;
}
.pq-cols {
  display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr;
  gap: 0.4rem; padding: 0.5rem;
}
.pq-col {
  border-radius: 6px; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid;
}
.pq-col-head {
  padding: 0.3rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.65rem; font-weight: 800;
  text-align: center; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em;
}
.pq-col-a .pq-col-head { background: #d6e2fb; color: #2747b0; border-bottom: 1px solid #b6c9ee; }
.pq-col-b .pq-col-head { background: #cdeedd; color: #1f6443; border-bottom: 1px solid #abdcc3; }
.pq-col-c .pq-col-head { background: #fbe3cd; color: #8a4a1c; border-bottom: 1px solid #f2cba3; }
.pq-col-a { border-color: #b6c9ee; background: #eef3fe; }
.pq-col-b { border-color: #abdcc3; background: #e9f8f0; }
.pq-col-c { border-color: #f2cba3; background: #fdf0e3; }
.pq-page {
  padding: 0.22rem 0.5rem; font-size: 0.6rem; color: #5b6b86;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.05); text-align: center;
}
.pq-page:last-child { border-bottom: none; }
.pq-footer {
  background: #11244a; color: #8fb0ff; padding: 0.5rem 1rem;
  font-size: 0.68rem; text-align: center; line-height: 1.4;
}

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   COMPRESSION / ENCODING VISUAL
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.encode-compare {
  width: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.8rem;
}
.enc-row {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.7rem;
}
.enc-label {
  width: 110px; font-size: 0.72rem; color: #5b6b86; flex-shrink: 0; font-weight: 600;
}
.enc-bar-track {
  flex: 1; height: 26px; background: #d3e0f4; border: 1px solid #c4d4ee; border-radius: 8px; overflow: visible;
}
.enc-bar {
  height: 100%; border-radius: 8px; display: flex; align-items: center;
  padding: 0 0.6rem; font-size: 0.65rem; font-weight: 700;
  transition: width 1s ease; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden;
}
.enc-csv  { background: #f2a159; color: #16181d; }
.enc-json { background: #f2b87a; color: #16181d; }
.enc-pq   { background: #4ab87d; color: #08120c; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   DUCKDB ARCHITECTURE VISUAL
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.duck-arch {
  width: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem;
}
.arch-layer {
  border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.7rem 1rem;
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.8rem;
  border: 1px solid;
}
.arch-icon { font-size: 1.4rem; flex-shrink: 0; }
.arch-text {}
.arch-text .arch-name { font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 800; margin-bottom: 0.1rem; }
.arch-text .arch-desc { font-size: 0.7rem; color: #5b6b86; }
.arch-l1 { background: #eef3fe; border-color: #b6c9ee; }
.arch-l1 .arch-name { color: #2747b0; }
.arch-l2 { background: #eef3fe; border-color: #b6c9ee; }
.arch-l2 .arch-name { color: #2747b0; }
.arch-l3 { background: #e9f8f0; border-color: #abdcc3; }
.arch-l3 .arch-name { color: #2c7a52; }
.arch-l4 { background: #fdf0e3; border-color: #f2cba3; }
.arch-l4 .arch-name { color: #b9692a; }
.arch-l5 { background: #fdf0e3; border-color: #f2cba3; }
.arch-l5 .arch-name { color: #b9692a; }
.arch-arrow { text-align: center; color: #8595b0; font-size: 0.9rem; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   OLTP vs OLAP SPLIT VISUAL
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.trade-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 0.8rem; width: 100%; }
.trade-card { border-radius: 8px; padding: 1rem 1.1rem; border: 1px solid; background: #ffffff; }
.trade-card.pro { background: #e9f8f0; border-color: #abdcc3; }
.trade-card.con { background: #fdf0e3; border-color: #f2cba3; }
.trade-card.neu { background: #eef3fe; border-color: #b6c9ee; }
.trade-card h4 { font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; margin-bottom: 0.6rem; }
.trade-card.pro h4 { color: #2c7a52; }
.trade-card.con h4 { color: #b9692a; }
.trade-card.neu h4 { color: #2747b0; }
.trade-card ul { padding-left: 1.1rem; font-size: 0.8rem; color: #33445f; }
.trade-card li { margin-bottom: 0.28rem; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   EXPERIMENT RESULTS VISUAL
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.exp-grid { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.6rem; width: 100%; }
.exp-row {
  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.6rem;
}
.exp-name { width: 160px; font-size: 0.72rem; color: #33445f; font-weight: 600; flex-shrink: 0; }
.exp-bars { flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 3px; }
.exp-bar-wrap { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.4rem; }
.exp-bar-label { width: 52px; font-size: 0.62rem; color: #8595b0; text-align: right; }
.exp-bar-track { flex: 1; height: 12px; background: #d3e0f4; border: 1px solid #c4d4ee; border-radius: 6px; overflow: hidden; }
.exp-bar { height: 100%; border-radius: 6px; }
.eb-native { background: #3b62db; }
.eb-direct { background: #4ab87d; }

.size-compare { display: flex; gap: 1rem; margin-top: 0.8rem; }
.size-box {
  flex: 1; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.8rem; text-align: center; border: 1px solid;
}
.size-box.native { background: #eef3fe; border-color: #b6c9ee; }
.size-box.direct { background: #e9f8f0; border-color: #abdcc3; }
.size-box .sz-num { font-size: 1.6rem; font-weight: 700; }
.size-box.native .sz-num { color: #2747b0; }
.size-box.direct .sz-num { color: #2c7a52; }
.size-box .sz-label { font-size: 0.7rem; color: #5b6b86; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   FULL-WIDTH INTERLUDE — solid blue band
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.interlude {
  padding: 6rem 4rem; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
  background: #3b62db;
}
.interlude-inner { max-width: 700px; text-align: center; }
.interlude-inner .int-label {
  font-size: 0.72rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.18em;
  color: #cdd8ff; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 1rem;
}
.interlude-inner .int-quote {
  font-size: clamp(1.2rem,2.4vw,1.85rem); font-weight: 600; color: #ffffff;
  line-height: 1.45; margin-bottom: 1rem;
}
.interlude-inner .int-sub { font-size: 0.92rem; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.85); line-height: 1.7; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   FINAL SUMMARY
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.final-section {
  padding: 8rem 4rem;
  background: linear-gradient(180deg, #dceafb 0%, #e9f1fd 100%);
  border-top: 1px solid #3b62db;
}
.final-section h2 {
  font-size: clamp(1.9rem,3.6vw,3rem); font-weight: 700; color: #16263f;
  letter-spacing: -0.02em; margin-bottom: 3rem; text-align: center;
}
.final-section h2 span { color: #3b62db; }
.takeaway-grid {
  display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(300px,1fr));
  gap: 1rem; max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto;
}
.tk-card {
  background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #c4d4ee;
  border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.5rem 1.6rem;
  transition: border-color 0.2s, transform 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s;
}
.tk-card:hover { border-color: #3b62db; transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 8px 22px rgba(59,98,219,0.12); }
.tk-num { font-size: 2.1rem; font-weight: 700; color: #c4d4ee; line-height:1; margin-bottom:0.7rem; }
.tk-card p { color: #33445f; font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.7; }
.tk-card p strong { color: #2747b0; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   TIMELINE (history)
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
.timeline {
  width: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0;
  position: relative; padding-left: 2rem;
}
.timeline::before {
  content: ''; position: absolute; left: 0.6rem; top: 0.5rem; bottom: 0.5rem;
  width: 2px; background: #3b62db;
  border-radius: 2px;
}
.tl-item {
  display: flex; gap: 1rem; align-items: flex-start;
  padding: 0.8rem 0; position: relative;
}
.tl-dot {
  position: absolute; left: -1.65rem; top: 1rem;
  width: 14px; height: 14px; border-radius: 50%;
  background: #3b62db; border: 2px solid #dde9fb;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.tl-year {
  font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 800; color: #3b62db;
  width: 40px; flex-shrink: 0; padding-top: 0.1rem;
}
.tl-content {}
.tl-title { font-size: 0.82rem; font-weight: 700; color: #16263f; margin-bottom: 0.2rem; }
.tl-desc  { font-size: 0.75rem; color: #5b6b86; line-height: 1.5; }

/* ══════════════════════════════════════════════
   RESPONSIVE
══════════════════════════════════════════════ */
@media(max-width:900px){
  .sticky-scrolly { flex-direction: column; }
  .sticky-left { position: relative; height: auto; min-height: 50vh; width: 100%; border-right: none; border-bottom: 1px solid #c4d4ee; }
  .sticky-panel { position: relative; opacity: 1; transform: none; overflow-y: visible; }
  .sticky-panel:not(.active){ display: none; }
  .sticky-panel > div { max-width: none; }
  .sticky-right { padding: 2rem 1.5rem; }
  .chapter-intro { padding: 3rem 1.5rem 2rem; flex-direction: column; gap: 1rem; }
  .chapter-intro .ch-num { font-size: 4rem; }
  .trade-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
  #side-nav { display: none; }
  .interlude { padding: 3rem 1.5rem; }
  .final-section { padding: 4rem 1.5rem; }
}
</style>

<!-- ═══════════ CHROME ═══════════ -->
<div id="progress-bar"></div>
<div id="side-nav"></div>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
     HERO
════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<section class="hero" id="sec-hero">
  <div class="hero-grid-bg"></div>
  <div class="hero-glow"></div>
  <div class="hero-inner">
    <div class="hero-eyebrow">● Data Storage Essentials</div>
    <h1>File Formats,<br>Parquet &amp;<br><em>DuckDB</em></h1>
    <p class="hero-sub">How data is stored on disk shapes how fast you can query it along with the space it takes up on the disk. From row-based CSVs to columnar Parquet files to embedded analytical databases every format has benefits and trade-offs.</p>
    <div class="hero-pills">
      <span class="hero-pill hero-pill-1">Storage Layouts</span>
      <span class="hero-pill hero-pill-2">Parquet Internals</span>
      <span class="hero-pill hero-pill-3">DuckDB Architecture</span>
      <span class="hero-pill hero-pill-4">When to Use What</span>
    </div>
    <div class="hero-scroll-cue">Scroll to explore<span>↓</span></div>
  </div>
</section>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
     CHAPTER 1 INTRO
════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="chapter-intro" id="sec-ch1">
  <div class="ch-num">01</div>
  <div class="ch-text">
    <div class="ch-tag">Foundations</div>
    <h2>File formats &amp; storage layouts</h2>
    <p>Before writing a single query, the data has to live somewhere. How a file arranges its bytes on disk — row by row, column by column, or a hybrid of both — determines what kinds of queries run fast and what kinds run slow.</p>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
     STICKY SECTION 1 — Storage Layouts
════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="sticky-scrolly" id="scrolly-1" data-chapter="1">

  <div class="sticky-left" id="left-1">

    <!-- Panel A: row vs column vs hybrid diagram -->
    <div class="sticky-panel active" id="panel-1-0">
      <div style="width:100%">
        <div style="font-size:0.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.14em;color:#6366f1;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:0.8rem;">Storage layout comparison</div>
        <div class="layout-compare">

          <!-- Original table reference -->
          <div class="layout-block" style="border-color:#94a3b8;">
            <div class="layout-head" style="background:#f1f5f9;color:#475569;border-bottom:1px solid #cbd5e1;">Original Table</div>
            <div style="background:#f8fafc;padding:0.5rem;">
              <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr);gap:3px;margin-bottom:3px;">
                <div class="cell" style="background:#2747b0;color:#fff;font-size:0.58rem;font-weight:700;">Col A</div>
                <div class="cell" style="background:#14532d;color:#fff;font-size:0.58rem;font-weight:700;">Col B</div>
                <div class="cell" style="background:#78350f;color:#fff;font-size:0.58rem;font-weight:700;">Col C</div>
                <div class="cell" style="background:#4c1d95;color:#fff;font-size:0.58rem;font-weight:700;">Col D</div>
              </div>
              <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr);gap:3px;margin-bottom:3px;">
                <div class="cell c-a">A1</div><div class="cell c-b">B1</div><div class="cell c-c">C1</div><div class="cell c-d">D1</div>
              </div>
              <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr);gap:3px;margin-bottom:3px;">
                <div class="cell c-a">A2</div><div class="cell c-b">B2</div><div class="cell c-c">C2</div><div class="cell c-d">D2</div>
              </div>
              <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr);gap:3px;">
                <div class="cell c-a">A3</div><div class="cell c-b">B3</div><div class="cell c-c">C3</div><div class="cell c-d">D3</div>
              </div>
            </div>
            <div style="padding:0.35rem 0.8rem;font-size:0.67rem;color:#475569;background:#f8fafc;">3 rows × 4 columns. Is the logical table before any storage layout is applied.</div>
          </div>

          <!-- Row-based -->
          <div class="layout-block" style="border-color:#fca5a5;">
            <div class="layout-head row-h"> Row-Based (CSV, JSON)</div>
            <div class="layout-grid lg-row" style="background:#fff5f5;padding:0.5rem;">
              <div class="cell c-a">A1</div><div class="cell c-b">B1</div><div class="cell c-c">C1</div><div class="cell c-d">D1</div>
              <div class="cell c-a">A2</div><div class="cell c-b">B2</div><div class="cell c-c">C2</div><div class="cell c-d">D2</div>
              <div class="cell c-a">A3</div><div class="cell c-b">B3</div><div class="cell c-c">C3</div><div class="cell c-d">D3</div>
            </div>
            <div style="padding:0.35rem 0.8rem;font-size:0.67rem;color:#b9692a;background:#fff5f5;">All columns per row stored together on disk</div>
          </div>

          <!-- Columnar -->
          <div class="layout-block" style="border-color:#86efac;">
            <div class="layout-head col-h">Columnar (Pure Columnar)</div>
            <div class="layout-grid lg-col" style="background:#f0fdf4;padding:0.5rem;">
              <div class="cell c-a">A1</div><div class="cell c-a">A2</div><div class="cell c-a">A3</div><div class="cell c-a">A4</div>
              <div class="cell c-b">B1</div><div class="cell c-b">B2</div><div class="cell c-b">B3</div><div class="cell c-b">B4</div>
              <div class="cell c-c">C1</div><div class="cell c-c">C2</div><div class="cell c-c">C3</div><div class="cell c-c">C4</div>
              <div class="cell c-d">D1</div><div class="cell c-d">D2</div><div class="cell c-d">D3</div><div class="cell c-d">D4</div>
            </div>
            <div style="padding:0.35rem 0.8rem;font-size:0.67rem;color:#2c7a52;background:#f0fdf4;">Each column stored contiguously — read only what you need</div>
          </div>

          <!-- Hybrid -->
          <div class="layout-block" style="border-color:#93c5fd;">
            <div class="layout-head hyb-h">Hybrid (Parquet) — Row Groups</div>
            <div style="background:#eff6ff;padding:0.5rem;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:4px;">
              <div style="font-size:0.62rem;color:#2747b0;font-weight:700;text-align:center;margin-bottom:2px;">Row Group 1</div>
              <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(8,1fr);gap:3px;">
                <div class="cell c-a">A1</div><div class="cell c-a">A2</div>
                <div class="cell c-b">B1</div><div class="cell c-b">B2</div>
                <div class="cell c-c">C1</div><div class="cell c-c">C2</div>
                <div class="cell c-d">D1</div><div class="cell c-d">D2</div>
              </div>
              <div style="font-size:0.62rem;color:#2747b0;font-weight:700;text-align:center;margin-top:4px;margin-bottom:2px;">Row Group 2</div>
              <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(8,1fr);gap:3px;">
                <div class="cell c-a">A3</div><div class="cell c-a">A4</div>
                <div class="cell c-b">B3</div><div class="cell c-b">B4</div>
                <div class="cell c-c">C3</div><div class="cell c-c">C4</div>
                <div class="cell c-d">D3</div><div class="cell c-d">D4</div>
              </div>
            </div>
            <div style="padding:0.35rem 0.8rem;font-size:0.67rem;color:#2747b0;background:#eff6ff;">Rows chunked into groups; within each group, stored columnarly</div>
          </div>

        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Panel B: format comparison table -->
    <div class="sticky-panel" id="panel-1-1">
      <div style="width:100%">
        <div style="font-size:0.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.14em;color:#3b62db;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:0.8rem;">Format comparison</div>
        <table class="vis-table">
          <thead>
            <tr>
              <th>Feature</th>
              <th>CSV</th>
              <th>JSON</th>
              <th>Parquet</th>
            </tr>
          </thead>
          <tbody>
            <tr><td>Layout</td><td class="cell-mid">Row</td><td class="cell-mid">Row</td><td class="cell-good">Hybrid</td></tr>
            <tr><td>Human-readable</td><td class="cell-good">Yes</td><td class="cell-good">Yes</td><td class="cell-bad">No</td></tr>
            <tr><td>Built-in compression</td><td class="cell-bad">No</td><td class="cell-bad">No</td><td class="cell-good">Yes</td></tr>
            <tr><td>Schema embedded</td><td class="cell-bad">No</td><td class="cell-mid">Partial</td><td class="cell-good">Yes</td></tr>
            <tr><td>Query efficiency</td><td class="cell-bad">Low</td><td class="cell-bad">Low</td><td class="cell-good">High</td></tr>
            <tr><td>Typical file size</td><td class="cell-mid">Medium</td><td class="cell-bad">Large</td><td class="cell-good">Small</td></tr>
            <tr><td>Best for</td><td class="cell-mid">Simple exports</td><td class="cell-mid">APIs / config</td><td class="cell-good">Analytics</td></tr>
          </tbody>
        </table>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Panel C: encoding / size comparison bars -->
    <div class="sticky-panel" id="panel-1-2">
      <div style="width:100%">
        <div style="font-size:0.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.14em;color:#3b62db;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:0.9rem;">Relative file size — same dataset</div>
        <div class="encode-compare">
          <div class="enc-row">
            <div class="enc-label">JSON</div>
            <div class="enc-bar-track">
              <div class="enc-bar enc-json" style="width:100%;color:#000;">1,834,260 bytes</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="enc-row">
            <div class="enc-label">CSV</div>
            <div class="enc-bar-track">
              <div class="enc-bar enc-csv" style="width:45%;color:#000;">829,667 bytes</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="enc-row">
            <div class="enc-label">Parquet</div>
            <div class="enc-bar-track">
              <div class="enc-bar enc-pq" style="width:18%;position:relative;overflow:visible;">
  <span style="position:absolute;left:0.4rem;top:50%;transform:translateY(-50%);white-space:nowrap;color:#000;font-weight:700;font-size:0.65rem;">327,738 bytes</span>
</div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div style="margin-top:1.1rem;background:#dbeafe;border:1px solid #93c5fd;border-radius:10px;padding:0.8rem 1rem;">
          <div style="font-size:0.72rem;color:#2747b0;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:0.3rem;">Why is Parquet so much smaller?</div>
          <div style="font-size:0.75rem;color:#2747b0;line-height:1.6;">
            Columnar storage groups similar values together, making compression extremely effective. Parquet also uses dictionary encoding, replacing repeated values like <code style="background:#bfdbfe;border-radius:3px;padding:0.05em 0.3em;">"California"</code> with a tiny integer code across all occurrences.
          </div>
        </div>
        <div style="margin-top:0.8rem;display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr 1fr;gap:0.5rem;text-align:center;">
          <div style="background:#dcfce7;border-radius:8px;padding:0.5rem;border:1px solid #86efac;">
            <div style="font-size:0.7rem;font-weight:700;color:#2c7a52;">Parquet</div>
            <div style="font-size:1.1rem;font-weight:700;color:#2c7a52;">1×</div>
            <div style="font-size:0.65rem;color:#64748b;">baseline</div>
          </div>
          <div style="background:#fef9c3;border-radius:8px;padding:0.5rem;border:1px solid #fde047;">
            <div style="font-size:0.7rem;font-weight:700;color:#b9692a;">CSV</div>
            <div style="font-size:1.1rem;font-weight:700;color:#b9692a;">2.5×</div>
            <div style="font-size:0.65rem;color:#64748b;">larger</div>
          </div>
          <div style="background:#fee2e2;border-radius:8px;padding:0.5rem;border:1px solid #fca5a5;">
            <div style="font-size:0.7rem;font-weight:700;color:#b9692a;">JSON</div>
            <div style="font-size:1.1rem;font-weight:700;color:#b9692a;">5.6×</div>
            <div style="font-size:0.65rem;color:#64748b;">larger</div>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div><!-- /sticky-left -->

  <div class="sticky-right">

    <div class="step" data-panel="1-0">
      <div class="step-tag">1.1 · Storage Layouts</div>
      <h3>How a file stores its bytes changes everything</h3>
      <p>A main distinction between file storage tupes is how the data is grouped, mainly <em style="color:#8a00c2;">row by row</em> or <em style="color:#8a00c2;">column by column</em>. This different impacts what kinds of queries and tasks perform more efficiently.</p>
      <p>A <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">row-based</strong> format like CSV or JSON stores all of a record's fields together. Reading one row is fast, but reading a single column across millions of rows means touching every row's data on disk, even the columns you don't care about.</p>
      <p>A <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">columnar</strong> format stores all values from one column together. Analytical queries that compute aggregates over one or two columns out of dozens skip the irrelevant columns entirely, the disk reads are far smaller and thus more efficient.</p>
      <p><strong style="color:#8a00c2;">Parquet</strong> uses a hybrid: it partitions rows into large <em style="color:#8a00c2;">row groups</em> (typically 100K–1M rows each) for large datasets, and within each group stores data columnarly. This gives columnar compression benefits while keeping related rows together for efficient scanning.</p>
      <div class="badge-row">
        <span class="badge br">Row-based</span>
        <span class="badge bg">Columnar</span>
        <span class="badge bi">Hybrid</span>
        <span class="badge bi">Row Groups</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="step" data-panel="1-1">
      <div class="step-tag">1.2 · Formats Compared</div>
      <h3>CSV, JSON, and Parquet serve different purposes</h3>
      <p><strong style="color:#8a00c2;">CSV</strong> is the simplest and typically most standard file type. It is a plain-text file where every value is separated by a comma representing each row at a time. Anyone can open it in a spreadsheet and read it. But it has no schema (a formal record of column names and their data types), no type information, no compression, and the database has to infer everything.</p>
      <p><strong style="color:#8a00c2;">JSON</strong> is more expressive, it supports nested objects and arrays, making it ideal for APIs and configuration. But nesting means the structure is irregular, which makes bulk analysis slow and produces large files. JSON is the least efficient storage format for analytics and typically the largest in size.</p>
      <p><strong style="color:#8a00c2;">Parquet</strong> is purpose-built for analytical workloads and storing information efficiently (compactly). It is not human-readable, in binary format but it stores the full schema in its footer, supports built-in column-level compression, and enables query engines to skip entire row groups that can't match a filter using stored statistics (located in the footer of the file).</p>
      <div class="badge-row">
        <span class="badge bg">CSV: simple &amp; portable</span>
        <span class="badge ba">JSON: flexible &amp; verbose</span>
        <span class="badge bi">Parquet: fast &amp; compact</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="step" data-panel="1-2">
      <div class="step-tag">1.3 · Compression &amp; Encoding</div>
      <h3>Columnar storage compresses information more efficiently</h3>
      <p>The graphic on the left comes from a real experiment comparing the same dataset stored in all three formats. Each format stores the exact same information from the <a href="https://openpolicing.stanford.edu/" target="_blank" style="color:#3b62db;text-decoration:underline;">Stanford Open Policing Project</a> <a href="references.qmd" target="_blank" style="color:#3b62db;text-decoration:underline;">(Pierson et al., 2023)</a> dataset from Los Angeles. However, the difference is striking in file size: the Parquet file is over 5× smaller than JSON and nearly 2.5× smaller than CSV.</p>
      <p>This happens for two reasons. First, columnar grouping means similar values sit next to each other in the file, <a href="https://parquet.apache.org/docs/file-format/data-pages/compression/" target="_blank" style="color:#3b62db;text-decoration:underline;"> compression algorithms like Snappy or Zstd</a> <a href="references.qmd" target="_blank" style="color:#3b62db;text-decoration:underline;"> (Apache Parquet, 2026)</a> exploit this repetition extremely well. Second, Parquet uses <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">dictionary encoding</strong>: if a string column contains the same 50 city names repeated millions of times, Parquet replaces each string with a compact integer and stores a single dictionary. The savings compound to make a strong disparity.</p>
      <p>Smaller files mean fewer bytes read from disk, which is usually the primary bottleneck in data analysis. Parquet's compression is not just a storage benefit, it is a query performance benefit too.</p>
      <div class="badge-row">
        <span class="badge bg">Dictionary encoding</span>
        <span class="badge bi">Snappy / Zstd compression</span>
        <span class="badge bs">Less disk I/O</span>
        <span class="badge ba">Schema in footer</span>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>
</div><!-- /scrolly-1 -->

<!-- ═══════════ INTERLUDE ═══════════ -->
<div class="interlude">
  <div class="interlude-inner">
    <div class="int-label">Fun historical insight</div>
    <div class="int-quote">Parquet was actually made by Twitter, and Google aided in its creation.</div>
    <div class="int-sub">Parquet's origin story actually traces back to a 2010 Google research paper called <a href="https://static.googleusercontent.com/media/research.google.com/en//pubs/archive/36632.pdf" target="_blank" style="color:#ffffff;text-decoration:underline;">"Dremel: Interactive Analysis of Web-Scale Datasets"</a> <a href="references.qmd" target="_blank" style="color:#ffffff;text-decoration:underline;">(Melnik et al., 2010)</a>, which introduced the idea of representing nested data in a columnar format. This trick let Google query massive datasets interactively instead of waiting on slow batch jobs.Three years later, in 2013, two unlikely co-conspirators Twitter and Cloudera joined forces and built Parquet together and donated it to the Apache Software Foundation, making it free and open for anyone to use. Twitter needed to crunch enormous volumes of data fast, and Cloudera was deep in the business of helping companies do exactly that at scale. Today Parquet is widely known as a open source file storage format purpose-built for analytical OLAP-style workloads, heavy on reads and aggregations, over huge piles of data, and it's remarkably space-efficient.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
     CHAPTER 2 INTRO
════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="chapter-intro" id="sec-ch2">
  <div class="ch-num">02</div>
  <div class="ch-text">
    <div class="ch-tag">Deep Dive</div>
    <h2>Inside a Parquet file</h2>
    <p>Parquet's speed comes from its internal structure. Understanding how a Parquet file is laid out on disk explains why query engines can skip irrelevant data without reading it.</p>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
     STICKY SECTION 2 — Parquet Internals
════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="sticky-scrolly" id="scrolly-2" data-chapter="2">

  <div class="sticky-left" id="left-2">

    <!-- Panel: Parquet file structure -->
    <div class="sticky-panel active" id="panel-2-0">
      <div style="width:100%">
        <div style="font-size:0.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.14em;color:#3b62db;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:0.8rem;">Internal layout of a .parquet file</div>
        <div class="parquet-struct">
          <div class="pq-magic">PAR1  (magic bytes — identifies Parquet format)</div>

          <!-- Row Group 1 -->
          <div class="pq-rg">
            <div class="pq-rg-label">Row Group 1 &nbsp;(e.g. rows 0 – 999,999)</div>
            <div class="pq-cols">
              <div class="pq-col pq-col-a">
                <div class="pq-col-head">Col A</div>
                <div class="pq-page" style="background:#dbeafe;">Dict Page</div>
                <div class="pq-page">Data Page 1</div>
                <div class="pq-page">Data Page 2</div>
              </div>
              <div class="pq-col pq-col-b">
                <div class="pq-col-head">Col B</div>
                <div class="pq-page" style="background:#bbf7d0;">Dict Page</div>
                <div class="pq-page">Data Page 1</div>
                <div class="pq-page">Data Page 2</div>
              </div>
              <div class="pq-col pq-col-c">
                <div class="pq-col-head">Col C</div>
                <div class="pq-page" style="background:#fde68a;">Dict Page</div>
                <div class="pq-page">Data Page 1</div>
                <div class="pq-page">Data Page 2</div>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <!-- Row Group 2 -->
          <div class="pq-rg" style="margin-top:4px;">
            <div class="pq-rg-label">Row Group 2 &nbsp;(e.g. rows 1,000,000 – 1,999,999)</div>
            <div class="pq-cols">
              <div class="pq-col pq-col-a">
                <div class="pq-col-head">Col A</div>
                <div class="pq-page" style="background:#dbeafe;">Dict Page</div>
                <div class="pq-page">Data Page 1</div>
              </div>
              <div class="pq-col pq-col-b">
                <div class="pq-col-head">Col B</div>
                <div class="pq-page" style="background:#bbf7d0;">Dict Page</div>
                <div class="pq-page">Data Page 1</div>
              </div>
              <div class="pq-col pq-col-c">
                <div class="pq-col-head">Col C</div>
                <div class="pq-page" style="background:#fde68a;">Dict Page</div>
                <div class="pq-page">Data Page 1</div>
              </div>
            </div>
            <div style="background:#f8faff;padding:0.5rem 1rem;display:flex;gap:1.2rem;font-size:0.65rem;color:#475569;border-top:1px solid #bfdbfe;flex-wrap:wrap;">
            <div><span style="background:#dbeafe;border-radius:3px;padding:0.1em 0.4em;font-weight:700;">Dict Page</span>: stores the lookup table of unique values. Like a dictionary that translates the actual data to simpler forms like integers if not already an integer.</div>
            <div><span style="background:#e2e8f0;border-radius:3px;padding:0.1em 0.4em;font-weight:700;">Data Page</span>: a group of data entries, stores the actual column values (as integer codes if dict-encoded)</div>
          </div>
          </div>

          <div class="pq-footer">
            FOOTER — schema, row group byte offsets, statistics (min/max per column per row group), encodings
          </div>
          <div class="pq-magic">PAR1  (magic bytes — end of file)</div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Panel: row group skipping visual -->
    <div class="sticky-panel" id="panel-2-1">
      <div style="width:100%">
        <div style="font-size:0.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.14em;color:#f59e0b;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:0.8rem;">Row group skipping in action</div>

        <div style="font-size:0.78rem;color:#334155;margin-bottom:0.6rem;">Query: <code style="background:#dbeafe;border:1px solid #93c5fd;color:#2747b0;padding:0.1em 0.4em;border-radius:4px;">WHERE date &gt; '2023-06-01'</code></div>

        <!-- RG cards -->
        <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:0.4rem;">
          <!-- skipped -->
          <div style="border-radius:10px;border:1px solid #fca5a5;background:#fff5f5;padding:0.6rem 0.9rem;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;">
            <div>
              <div style="font-size:0.72rem;font-weight:700;color:#b9692a;">Row Group 1</div>
              <div style="font-size:0.65rem;color:#94a3b8;">date: 2021-01-01 → 2021-12-31</div>
            </div>
            <div style="background:#fee2e2;border-radius:6px;padding:0.2rem 0.6rem;font-size:0.65rem;font-weight:800;color:#b9692a;">⏭ SKIPPED</div>
          </div>
          <div style="border-radius:10px;border:1px solid #fca5a5;background:#fff5f5;padding:0.6rem 0.9rem;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;">
            <div>
              <div style="font-size:0.72rem;font-weight:700;color:#b9692a;">Row Group 2</div>
              <div style="font-size:0.65rem;color:#94a3b8;">date: 2022-01-01 → 2022-12-31</div>
            </div>
            <div style="background:#fee2e2;border-radius:6px;padding:0.2rem 0.6rem;font-size:0.65rem;font-weight:800;color:#b9692a;">⏭ SKIPPED</div>
          </div>
          <div style="border-radius:10px;border:1px solid #fca5a5;background:#fff5f5;padding:0.6rem 0.9rem;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;">
            <div>
              <div style="font-size:0.72rem;font-weight:700;color:#b9692a;">Row Group 3</div>
              <div style="font-size:0.65rem;color:#94a3b8;">date: 2023-01-01 → 2023-05-30</div>
            </div>
            <div style="background:#fee2e2;border-radius:6px;padding:0.2rem 0.6rem;font-size:0.65rem;font-weight:800;color:#b9692a;">⏭ SKIPPED</div>
          </div>
          <!-- read -->
          <div style="border-radius:10px;border:1px solid #86efac;background:#f0fdf4;padding:0.6rem 0.9rem;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;">
            <div>
              <div style="font-size:0.72rem;font-weight:700;color:#2c7a52;">Row Group 4</div>
              <div style="font-size:0.65rem;color:#2c7a52;">date: 2023-06-01 → 2023-12-31</div>
            </div>
            <div style="background:#dcfce7;border-radius:6px;padding:0.2rem 0.6rem;font-size:0.65rem;font-weight:800;color:#2c7a52;">✓ READ</div>
          </div>
          <div style="border-radius:10px;border:1px solid #86efac;background:#f0fdf4;padding:0.6rem 0.9rem;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:space-between;">
            <div>
              <div style="font-size:0.72rem;font-weight:700;color:#2c7a52;">Row Group 5</div>
              <div style="font-size:0.65rem;color:#2c7a52;">date: 2024-01-01 → 2024-12-31</div>
            </div>
            <div style="background:#dcfce7;border-radius:6px;padding:0.2rem 0.6rem;font-size:0.65rem;font-weight:800;color:#2c7a52;">✓ READ</div>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div style="margin-top:0.8rem;background:#fef9c3;border:1px solid #fde047;border-radius:9px;padding:0.6rem 0.8rem;font-size:0.72rem;color:#713f12;">
          The footer's per-row-group min/max statistics let the engine skip 3 of 5 row groups before reading a single data byte.
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Panel: Parquet advantages checklist -->
    <div class="sticky-panel" id="panel-2-2">
      <div style="width:100%">
        <div style="font-size:0.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.14em;color:#3b62db;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:0.8rem;">Parquet advantages</div>
        <div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:0.5rem;">

          <div style="background:#dbeafe;border:1px solid #93c5fd;border-radius:10px;padding:0.7rem 0.9rem;display:flex;gap:0.7rem;align-items:flex-start;">
            <div style="font-size:1.1rem;flex-shrink:0;"></div>
            <div>
              <div style="font-size:0.76rem;font-weight:800;color:#2747b0;margin-bottom:0.2rem;">Columnar grouped storage</div>
              <div style="font-size:0.72rem;color:#2747b0;line-height:1.5;">Reads only the columns needed: analytical queries touch a fraction of the data</div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div style="background:#dcfce7;border:1px solid #86efac;border-radius:10px;padding:0.7rem 0.9rem;display:flex;gap:0.7rem;align-items:flex-start;">
            <div style="font-size:1.1rem;flex-shrink:0;"></div>
            <div>
              <div style="font-size:0.76rem;font-weight:800;color:#2c7a52;margin-bottom:0.2rem;">Compression efficiency</div>
              <div style="font-size:0.72rem;color:#166534;line-height:1.5;">Built-in Snappy / Zstd compression: Parquet files are consistently 3–10× smaller than equivalent CSVs</div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div style="background:#ede9fe;border:1px solid #c4b5fd;border-radius:10px;padding:0.7rem 0.9rem;display:flex;gap:0.7rem;align-items:flex-start;">
            <div style="font-size:1.1rem;flex-shrink:0;"></div>
            <div>
              <div style="font-size:0.76rem;font-weight:800;color:#5b6b86;margin-bottom:0.2rem;">Dictionary encoding</div>
              <div style="font-size:0.72rem;color:#4c1d95;line-height:1.5;">Replaces repeated string values with compact integer codes: huge savings on categorical columns</div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div style="background:#fef9c3;border:1px solid #fde047;border-radius:10px;padding:0.7rem 0.9rem;display:flex;gap:0.7rem;align-items:flex-start;">
            <div style="font-size:1.1rem;flex-shrink:0;"></div>
            <div>
              <div style="font-size:0.76rem;font-weight:800;color:#b9692a;margin-bottom:0.2rem;">Schema embedded in footer</div>
              <div style="font-size:0.72rem;color:#78350f;line-height:1.5;">Column names, types, and statistics are stored in the file itself: no external schema needed</div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div style="background:#f0fdf4;border:1px solid #86efac;border-radius:10px;padding:0.7rem 0.9rem;display:flex;gap:0.7rem;align-items:flex-start;">
            <div style="font-size:1.1rem;flex-shrink:0;"></div>
            <div>
              <div style="font-size:0.76rem;font-weight:800;color:#2c7a52;margin-bottom:0.2rem;">Schema evolution</div>
              <div style="font-size:0.72rem;color:#166534;line-height:1.5;">Add or remove columns without rewriting the entire file: new tools can simply ignore unknown columns</div>
            </div>
          </div>

        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div><!-- /sticky-left -->

  <div class="sticky-right">

    <div class="step" data-panel="2-0">
      <div class="step-tag">2.1 · File Structure</div>
      <h3>A Parquet file has more than just data</h3>
      <p>Every Parquet file starts and ends with the four magic bytes <code>PAR1</code>. This simply identify's the file on the computer as a Parquet file. Between the magic bytes, the data is divided into <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">row groups</strong>: horizontal partitions, each containing a chunk of rows. Within each row group, every column's data is stored as its own contiguous <em style="color:#8a00c2;">column chunk</em>, further split into pages. Pages are the smallest unit of storage in Parquet. Each page holds a batch of values for one column, and a column chunk is simply a sequence of these pages read one after another.</p>
      <p>The <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">footer</strong> at the end of the file is critical and unique to parquet files compared to JSON or CSV. It holds the full schema (column names and data types), the byte offset (the exact position in the file, measured in bytes from the start, where each row group begins) of every row group, and per-column statistics including minimum and maximum values. A query engine reads the footer first and uses it to build a plan before reading any actual data.</p> 
      <p>The footer is created automatically when the Parquet file is written. The software writing the file (such as DuckDB, Spark, or the R arrow package) tracks the schema, byte offsets, and min/max statistics as it writes each row group, then writes the metadata into the footer as the very last step before closing the file.</p>
      <p>This design means a tool like DuckDB can open a Parquet file, read the footer in milliseconds, and immediately know which row groups can be skipped for a given query filter,without touching any data pages.</p>
      <div class="badge-row">
        <span class="badge bi">PAR1 magic bytes</span>
        <span class="badge bg">Row groups</span>
        <span class="badge bp">Column chunks</span>
        <span class="badge ba">Footer metadata</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="step" data-panel="2-1">
      <div class="step-tag">2.2 · Predicate Pushdown</div>
      <h3>Row group skipping and why it is beneficial</h3>
      <p>Parquet's footer stores the minimum and maximum value of every column in every row group, which applies to any data type. This enables a technique called <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">predicate pushdown</strong>: pushing a filter condition down into the storage layer so data that can't possibly match is never loaded.</p>
      <p>Consider <code>WHERE date &gt; '2023-06-01'</code> against a file with five row groups. The footer reveals row groups 1–3 have max dates before the cutoff. The engine skips them entirely. Only groups 4 and 5 are read. If you have 100 row groups and the filter eliminates 90, you read 10% of the file.</p>
      <p>This is a form of metadata-driven pruning. It doesn't require a B+ tree index since the statistics in the footer are sufficient. It is one of the main reasons Parquet files can be queried efficiently by tools that don't load the data into a database first.</p>
      <div class="badge-row">
        <span class="badge bi">Predicate pushdown</span>
        <span class="badge bg">Min/max statistics</span>
        <span class="badge bs">Row group pruning</span>
        <span class="badge ba">No index needed</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="step" data-panel="2-2">
      <div class="step-tag">2.3 · When to Use Parquet</div>
      <h3>Parquet Benefits</h3>
      <p>Use Parquet when your workflow is analytical. In other words, when your frequent queries aggregate, filter, or summarize across many rows. It outperforms CSV and JSON on these workloads not by a small margin but by an order of magnitude, because it reads far fewer bytes from disk.</p>
      <p>Parquet's <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">universality</strong> is also a major advantage. It works seamlessly across the entire modern data stack — Spark, DuckDB, Pandas, Polars, BigQuery, Snowflake, Athena, and Hive all read and write Parquet natively, as do R packages like <code>arrow</code> and <code>dplyr</code> via <code>dbplyr</code>. Choosing Parquet as your storage format means you can utilize many various tools.</p>
      <p>The only reason to prefer CSV or JSON over Parquet is human readability and editability. If someone needs to open the file in a text editor or spreadsheet, CSV would be better. If you're building an API response payload, JSON would be better. If you're storing data for analysis, Parquet is almost always the better choice.</p>
      <div class="badge-row">
        <span class="badge bg">Analytical workloads</span>
        <span class="badge bi">Universal compatibility</span>
        <span class="badge bs">Schema evolution</span>
        <span class="badge br">Not human-readable</span>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>
</div><!-- /scrolly-2 -->

<!-- ═══════════ INTERLUDE 2 ═══════════ -->
<div class="interlude">
  <div class="interlude-inner">
    <div class="int-label">So who actually queries Parquet?</div>
    <div class="int-quote">DuckDB, known as the "SQLite for analytics" is a full SQL engine that runs entirely inside your process, with no server to configure.</div>
    <div class="int-sub">DuckDB can query Parquet files directly on disk with no load step. It combines Parquet's columnar storage efficiency with full SQL expressiveness and the simplicity of an embedded database.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
     CHAPTER 3 INTRO
════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="chapter-intro" id="sec-ch3">
  <div class="ch-num">03</div>
  <div class="ch-text">
    <div class="ch-tag">Embedded Analytics</div>
    <h2>DuckDB</h2>
    <p>DuckDB is an open-source, in-process analytical SQL database. It brings the full capabilities of SQL to local data files — CSV, Parquet, JSON — without requiring a server, users, passwords, or any infrastructure.</p>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
     STICKY SECTION 3 — DuckDB
════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="sticky-scrolly" id="scrolly-3" data-chapter="3">

  <div class="sticky-left" id="left-3">

    <!-- Panel: DuckDB architecture layers -->
    <div class="sticky-panel active" id="panel-3-0">
      <div style="width:100%">
        <div style="font-size:0.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.14em;color:#3b62db;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:0.8rem;">DuckDB — how it fits in your workflow</div>
        <div class="duck-arch">
          <div class="arch-layer arch-l1">
            <div class="arch-icon"></div>
            <div class="arch-text">
              <div class="arch-name">Your Program (Python / R / CLI)</div>
              <div class="arch-desc">You write SQL queries and receive results as data frames or tables</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="arch-arrow">↕ in-process (no network hop)</div>
          <div class="arch-layer arch-l2">
            <div class="arch-icon"></div>
            <div class="arch-text">
              <div class="arch-name">DuckDB Engine</div>
              <div class="arch-desc">SQL parser → optimizer → vectorized execution engine</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="arch-arrow">↕ reads directly</div>
          <div class="arch-layer arch-l3">
            <div class="arch-icon"></div>
            <div class="arch-text">
              <div class="arch-name">Local Files</div>
              <div class="arch-desc">Can read parquet files directly, no loading required</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="arch-arrow">— or writes to —</div>
          <div class="arch-layer arch-l4">
            <div class="arch-icon"></div>
            <div class="arch-text">
              <div class="arch-name">DuckDB Database (.duckdb)</div>
              <div class="arch-desc">Single file on disk. Persistent, columnar, compressed storage</div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div style="margin-top:0.8rem;background:#dbeafe;border:1px solid #93c5fd;border-radius:9px;padding:0.65rem 0.9rem;font-size:0.72rem;color:#2747b0;line-height:1.5;">
          No server. No users. No config. Install as a package and start querying.
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Panel: DuckDB vs MySQL vs PostgreSQL comparison -->
    <div class="sticky-panel" id="panel-3-1">
      <div style="width:100%">
        <div style="font-size:0.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.14em;color:#3b62db;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:0.8rem;">DuckDB vs. MySQL vs. PostgreSQL</div>
        <style>#panel-3-1 .vis-table td, #panel-3-1 .vis-table th { padding: 0.4rem 0.5rem; }</style>
        <table class="vis-table" style="font-size:0.7rem;table-layout:fixed;width:100%;">
          <thead>
            <tr>
              <th style="width:35%;">Property</th>
              <th style="width:20%;">MySQL</th>
              <th style="width:22%;">PostgreSQL</th>
              <th style="width:23%;">DuckDB</th>
            </tr>
          </thead>
          <tbody>
            <tr><td style="white-space:nowrap;">Architecture</td><td class="cell-good">Client-server</td><td class="cell-good">Client-server</td><td class="cell-good">In-process</td></tr>
            <tr><td style="white-space:nowrap;">Optimized for</td><td class="cell-good">OLTP</td><td class="cell-good">OLTP + OLAP</td><td class="cell-good">OLAP</td></tr>
            <tr><td style="white-space:nowrap;">Setup required</td><td class="cell-bad">Server + config</td><td class="cell-bad">Server + config</td><td class="cell-good">pip/install.packages</td></tr>
            <tr><td style="white-space:nowrap;">Storage layout</td><td class="cell-good">Row (B+ tree)</td><td class="cell-good">Row (heap)</td><td class="cell-good">Columnar</td></tr>
            <tr><td style="white-space:nowrap;">Query files directly</td><td class="cell-bad">No</td><td class="cell-bad">No</td><td class="cell-good">Yes (CSV/Parquet/JSON)</td></tr>
            <tr><td style="white-space:nowrap;">Best for</td><td class="cell-good">Web apps</td><td class="cell-good">Complex apps</td><td class="cell-good">Local analytics</td></tr>
          </tbody>
        </table>
        <div style="margin-top:0.8rem;background:#fef9c3;border:1px solid #fde047;border-radius:9px;padding:0.65rem 0.9rem;font-size:0.72rem;color:#713f12;line-height:1.5;">
          DuckDB is not a replacement for MySQL or PostgreSQL. It's a different tool for a different job: local analytical queries instead of multi-user transactional applications.
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Panel: query from parquet example -->
    <div class="sticky-panel" id="panel-3-2">
      <div style="width:100%">
        <div style="font-size:0.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:0.14em;color:#3b62db;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:0.8rem;">Querying files directly — no database needed</div>
        <div class="sql-block" style="font-size:0.79rem;">
<span class="cm">-- Query a Parquet file directly on disk</span><br>
<span class="kw">SELECT</span><br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;column_a,<br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;<span class="fn">SUM</span>(column_b) <span class="kw">AS</span> total<br>
<span class="kw">FROM</span> <span class="fn">read_parquet</span>(<span class="st">'data.parquet'</span>)<br>
<span class="kw">WHERE</span> column_a = <span class="st">'value'</span><br>
<span class="kw">GROUP BY</span> column_a;<br><br>
<span class="cm">-- Query ALL parquet files in a folder</span><br>
<span class="kw">SELECT</span> * <span class="kw">FROM</span> <span class="fn">read_parquet</span>(<span class="st">'folder/*.parquet'</span>);<br><br>
<span class="cm">-- Load into a persistent DuckDB database</span><br>
<span class="kw">CREATE TABLE</span> <span class="fn">stops</span> <span class="kw">AS</span><br>
&nbsp;&nbsp;<span class="kw">SELECT</span> * <span class="kw">FROM</span> <span class="fn">read_parquet</span>(<span class="st">'*.parquet'</span>);
        </div>
        <div style="margin-top:0.7rem;background:#dbeafe;border:1px solid #93c5fd;border-radius:9px;padding:0.65rem 0.9rem;font-size:0.72rem;color:#2747b0;line-height:1.5;">
          DuckDB reads the Parquet footer first, pushes predicates into the scan, and skips irrelevant row groups.
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div><!-- /sticky-left -->

  <div class="sticky-right">

    <div class="step" data-panel="3-0">
      <div class="step-tag">3.1 · What is DuckDB</div>
      <h3>An embedded SQL engine and no server required</h3>
      <p>DuckDB is described as "the SQLite for analytics." SQLite is the local SQL dialect and like SQLite, DuckDB is an <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">embedded, in-process database</strong>. It runs entirely inside your program rather than as a separate server. The entire engine is a single library you install as a package in Python or R, though DuckDB also ships a standalone CLI (command-line interface) you can run directly from the terminal without any host language. There is no authentication, no port to configure, and no connection to start.</p>
      <p>Unlike SQLite, which is optimized for transactional row-level operations just like MySQL, DuckDB is purpose-built for <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">analytical workloads</strong>. It uses a columnar execution engine, processes data in batches, and is optimized for queries that scan many rows, aggregate, group, filter, and join large datasets.</p>
      <p>A DuckDB database is a <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">single file</strong> on disk with a <code>.duckdb</code> extension. You can also use DuckDB in fully in-memory mode, where nothing is written to disk at all. Both modes give you full SQL expressiveness with analytical performance.</p>
      <div class="badge-row">
        <span class="badge bi">Embedded / in-process</span>
        <span class="badge bg">No server needed</span>
        <span class="badge bp">Columnar execution</span>
        <span class="badge ba">Single-file database</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="step" data-panel="3-1">
      <div class="step-tag">3.2 · DuckDB vs. Traditional SQL</div>
      <h3>A different kind of SQL database</h3>
      <p>MySQL and PostgreSQL are <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">client-server</strong> databases: a long-running server process manages shared access, handles concurrent transactions, and stores data in row-oriented storage. This architecture is excellent for web applications with many simultaneous users reading and writing small numbers of rows.</p>
      <p>DuckDB makes the opposite trade-offs. It is designed for <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">a single analyst or process</strong> running complex queries over large datasets. Because it runs in-process, there is no network overhead between the query engine and the query. The results come back as in-memory data frames, not network packets like with MySQL or PostgreSQL.</p>
      <p>The storage engine is also fundamentally different. MySQL stores table data in <a href="SQL_Scrolly.html#sec-ch3" target="_blank" style="color:#3b62db;text-decoration:underline;">B+ trees</a> (row-oriented). DuckDB stores data columnarly with vectorized execution, processing columns in batches of 2,048 values at a time. This is precisely what makes aggregation queries fast: the CPU can operate on contiguous arrays of values rather than jumping between rows.</p>
      <div class="badge-row">
        <span class="badge bi">Vectorized execution</span>
        <span class="badge bs">No network overhead</span>
        <span class="badge bg">Batch processing</span>
        <span class="badge ba">Single-user analytics</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="step" data-panel="3-2">
      <div class="step-tag">3.3 · Querying Files Directly</div>
      <h3>DuckDB can query Parquet files without loading them first</h3>
      <p>One of DuckDB's most powerful features is the ability to run SQL queries <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">directly against parquet files on disk</strong>, no database ingestion step required. The <code>read_parquet()</code> function accepts a file path (or a glob pattern like <code>*.parquet</code>) and exposes it as a queryable table.</p>
      <p>This is not just a convenience feature. DuckDB's Parquet reader exploits the file's footer statistics to implement <strong style="color:#8a00c2;">predicate pushdown</strong>, the same row group skipping described earlier. A filter in your SQL <code>WHERE</code> clause is pushed all the way into the file scan, skipping row groups that can't match. This means you get analytical warehouse behavior on local files.</p>
      <p>When you do want persistence, the <code>CREATE TABLE AS SELECT</code> pattern copies data into DuckDB's own columnar format, which is even faster for repeated queries. The choice between querying files directly and loading into a native database is a trade-off depending on the query and the frequency of the each type of querying.</p>
      <div class="badge-row">
        <span class="badge bi">read_parquet()</span>
        <span class="badge bg">Glob patterns</span>
        <span class="badge bs">Predicate pushdown</span>
        <span class="badge bp">CTAS for persistence</span>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>
</div><!-- /scrolly-3 -->



<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
     FINAL SUMMARY
════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<section class="final-section" id="sec-summary">
  <h2>Key <span>Insights</span></h2>
  <div class="takeaway-grid">
    <div class="tk-card">
      <div class="tk-num">01</div>
      <p><strong>Storage layout determines query performance.</strong> Row-based formats (CSV, JSON) store each record together: good for returning full rows, slow for scanning one column across millions. Columnar formats store each column together: excellent for analytical aggregations, less ideal for full-row retrieval.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="tk-card">
      <div class="tk-num">02</div>
      <p><strong>Parquet is a hybrid</strong>: rows are partitioned into large row groups, and within each group data is stored columnarly. This gives both compression efficiency and fast columnar scans. The footer stores schema and per-row-group statistics enabling predicate pushdown: row groups are skipped before any data is read.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="tk-card">
      <div class="tk-num">03</div>
      <p><strong>Parquet compresses dramatically better than CSV or JSON</strong> due to columnar grouping (similar values together) and dictionary encoding (repeating strings replaced with compact integer codes). The same dataset can be 3–10× smaller as Parquet, which means faster queries because less data is read from disk.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="tk-card">
      <div class="tk-num">04</div>
      <p><strong>DuckDB is an in-process analytical SQL database</strong>, install as a package, query immediately. A database is a single <code>.duckdb</code> file. It uses vectorized, columnar execution and processes data in batches of 2,048 values, making it fast for aggregation-heavy analytical queries.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="tk-card">
      <div class="tk-num">05</div>
      <p><strong>Once ingested into DuckDB, the source format is irrelevant.</strong> DuckDB normalizes CSV, Parquet, and JSON into its own internal columnar representation. Query performance converges regardless of source. Parquet is still the best source format because it ingests information faster and the file is smaller on disk.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="tk-card">
      <div class="tk-num">06</div>
      <p><strong>Native DuckDB and direct Parquet are both columnar but not the same.</strong> Parquet is a file format read on demand from disk. DuckDB ingests data into its own internal columnar storage. Once inside, the source format no longer matters. The trade-off is storage (DuckDB takes ~3× more space) versus speed (DuckDB is faster for repeated queries). Choose direct Parquet for one-off queries; choose native DuckDB when querying the same data frequently.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<script src="https://unpkg.com/scrollama@3.2.0/build/scrollama.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
"use strict";

/* ── PROGRESS BAR ── */
const bar = document.getElementById('progress-bar');
window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
  const s = window.scrollY, t = document.documentElement.scrollHeight - window.innerHeight;
  bar.style.width = (t > 0 ? s/t*100 : 0) + '%';
}, {passive:true});

/* ── SIDE NAV ── */
const navSections = [
  {id:'sec-hero',    label:'Intro'},
  {id:'sec-ch1',     label:'Ch 1: Storage Layouts'},
  {id:'scrolly-1',   label:'Row · Column · Hybrid'},
  {id:'sec-ch2',     label:'Ch 2: Parquet Internals'},
  {id:'scrolly-2',   label:'Structure · Skipping · Use Cases'},
  {id:'sec-ch3',     label:'Ch 3: DuckDB'},
  {id:'scrolly-3',   label:'Architecture · vs SQL · Files'},
  {id:'sec-ch4',     label:'Ch 4: Experiments'},
  {id:'scrolly-4',   label:'Source Format · Native vs Direct'},
  {id:'sec-summary', label:'Summary'},
];
const nav = document.getElementById('side-nav');
navSections.forEach(s => {
  const d = document.createElement('div');
  d.className = 'nav-dot'; d.dataset.id = s.id;
  d.innerHTML = `<span class="nav-tip">${s.label}</span>`;
  d.addEventListener('click', () => {
    const el = document.getElementById(s.id);
    if (el) el.scrollIntoView({behavior:'smooth', block:'start'});
  });
  nav.appendChild(d);
});
function updateNav(){
  navSections.forEach(s => {
    const el = document.getElementById(s.id), d = nav.querySelector(`[data-id="${s.id}"]`);
    if(!el||!d) return;
    const r = el.getBoundingClientRect();
    d.classList.toggle('active', r.top < window.innerHeight*0.55 && r.bottom > 0);
  });
}
window.addEventListener('scroll', updateNav, {passive:true});

/* ── PANEL SWITCHER ── */
function showPanel(panelId){
  const panel = document.getElementById('panel-' + panelId);
  if(!panel) return;
  const container = panel.closest('.sticky-left');
  if(!container) return;
  container.querySelectorAll('.sticky-panel').forEach(p => p.classList.remove('active'));
  panel.classList.add('active');
}

/* ── SCROLLAMA SETUP ── */
if(typeof scrollama !== 'undefined'){
  ['1','2','3','4'].forEach(ch => {
    const scrolly = document.getElementById('scrolly-' + ch);
    if(!scrolly) return;
    const steps = scrolly.querySelectorAll('.step');
    if(!steps.length) return;

    steps[0].classList.add('is-active');
    const firstPanel = steps[0].dataset.panel;
    if(firstPanel) showPanel(firstPanel);

    const scroller = scrollama();
    scroller.setup({
      step: '#scrolly-' + ch + ' .step',
      offset: 0.5,
    })
    .onStepEnter(({element}) => {
      steps.forEach(s => s.classList.remove('is-active'));
      element.classList.add('is-active');
      const panelId = element.dataset.panel;
      if(panelId) showPanel(panelId);
    });
    window.addEventListener('resize', scroller.resize);
  });
} else {
  document.querySelectorAll('.step').forEach(s => s.classList.add('is-active'));
  document.querySelectorAll('.sticky-panel').forEach(p => p.classList.add('active'));
}

updateNav();

})();
</script>
```
